extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb won from 6th-7th position with 200m left, finding gaps through terrain/wind positioning his depleted Lotto-Soudal missed.
tactical_patterns: PEREZ Anthony repeatedly outsprinted KOSNICHENKO on Cat climbs despite KOSNICHENKO sitting his wheel, revealing pure power differential over tactics.
AG2R's KOSNICHENKO attacked immediately after voluntarily returning from breakaway while teammate COUSIN had 3:40 gap, mystifying resource allocation.
EWAN waited until absolute last 200m from 6th-7th wheel, exploiting terrain/wind positioning despite losing lead-out man DEGENKOLB in stage 1.
ALAPHILIPPE holding yellow with only seconds margin before first summit finish suggests Deceuninck prioritizing stage over GC defense depth.

rider_signals.PEREZ Anthony: positive: Beat KOSNICHENKO in three separate KOM sprints through raw power, not positioning, suggesting underrated climbing speed
rider_signals.KOSNICHENKO Benoit: negative: Attacked solo despite teammate 3:40 ahead and lost three KOM sprints from advantageous wheel position
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: Won from 6th-7th at 200m without primary lead-out DEGENKOLB, indicating elite gap-finding ability independent of train
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: Commentary expects minimal GC defense tomorrow, suggesting team accepts jersey loss at first summit finish
